behaviors.description: Only files changed in the pull request are analyzed — unchanged files are not scanned.
behaviors.justification: A pure in-process mock can miss real failures in changed-file discovery (git diff/PR file listing pagination, rename handling, path normalization). The behavior crosses subprocess/filesystem/PR metadata boundaries, so minimum real-failure coverage is System.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp git repo with a base branch and PR branch where only a subset of files change (including rename and nested paths). Run the actual pipeline lint entrypoint as a subprocess exactly as CI does, then assert analyzer input contains only PR-changed files and excludes unchanged files.

behaviors.description: Findings are grouped by severity (error, warning, info) in the posted PR comment.
behaviors.justification: Mocking everything at unit level can hide contract mismatches between rule output, grouping logic, and comment renderer. No real OS/network/model dependency is required to verify correct grouped comment content, so Integration is sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Wire parser -> finding aggregator -> comment formatter using realistic multi-severity findings from multiple files, then assert generated PR comment body has distinct severity sections with correct membership and counts. Use a fake comment sink but real formatting pipeline.

behaviors.description: A pull request that touches 200 files completes analysis within the 10-minute Actions timeout.
behaviors.justification: This is a performance/SLO behavior dependent on real runtime characteristics (filesystem, subprocess startup, linter execution, CI-like resource limits). In-process mocks can trivially pass and hide timeout regressions.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real lint job as a subprocess on a generated fixture PR with 200 changed files and representative file sizes/rule mix, under CI-like CPU/memory limits. Measure wall-clock end-to-end completion and fail if it exceeds a safety threshold below 10 minutes (e.g., 9 minutes).

behaviors.description: When the linter process crashes on a malformed file, the remaining files are still analyzed and results posted.
behaviors.justification: Crash-recovery correctness depends on real subprocess failure modes and orchestration behavior. Mocking the linter call can hide signal/exit-code/stream handling bugs; real infra is needed.
behaviors.test_description: Prepare a PR fixture with one malformed file that causes the real linter subprocess to crash and several valid files. Execute the pipeline entrypoint as CI would, verify non-crashing files are still analyzed, and assert final posted comment includes surviving findings plus explicit crash note for the bad file.

behaviors.description: Duplicate findings on the same line from overlapping rules are deduplicated in the output.
behaviors.justification: The real failure boundary is usually deterministic dedup-key logic (file, line, rule/message normalization) inside one local aggregation component. No OS/network/subprocess/model dependency is required for minimum detection.
behaviors.test_description: Call the dedup function with crafted findings containing same file+line duplicates from different overlapping rules and near-duplicates with different lines/messages. Assert exact retained set, stable ordering, and no accidental collapse of distinct findings.
